label: College B2B Hustler

segment.context: Business or econ major who had the startup idea, relies on technical co-founder or no-code tools, focused on sales and go-to-market

goals.goal: find a tool that makes them look professional to potential B2B clients
set up something functional without writing code
find templates, examples, or pre-built solutions they can customize
verify other startups or YC companies use this tool as social proof

constraints: limited technical ability — needs no-code or low-code options
making purchasing decisions based on how impressive it looks to clients
often copying what they see YC or TechCrunch startups using
budget is personal savings or small grant money
needs to show progress to advisor or co-founder quickly
behavioral_rules: reads the hero section and tagline carefully — needs to understand what this does in 5 seconds
looks for 'templates' or 'examples' or 'use cases' in the navigation
clicks on customer logos or 'trusted by' sections
watches the product demo video if it's under 2 minutes
checks if there's a 'startup program' or 'startup discount'
reads case studies but only ones from companies with < 50 employees
signs up if there's a 'get started free' button prominently placed
gets confused by technical jargon — bounces if the page assumes developer knowledge
screenshots the pricing page to send to co-founder for discussion
abandons if the product feels enterprise-only based on language and design
checks Product Hunt or Twitter mentions before committing
deal_breakers: product requires coding to set up — no no-code option
no visible customer logos or social proof
pricing starts above $30/month with no free tier
website uses too much technical jargon without explanation
no templates or quickstart that a non-developer can use
product demo requires scheduling a call
voice_sample: I'm trying to build a lead gen tool for real estate agents and I need something that looks legit when I demo it to potential customers next week. I don't code — my co-founder does the technical stuff but he's slammed with finals right now. If I can get a landing page and basic workflow running by Thursday using your templates, you've got a customer. But if I need to write Python to make this work, I'm going to Bubble or Webflow instead. Also, do any YC companies use this? That matters to me.
comparison_products: Notion vs Airtable for internal ops
